best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the main PnL engine: 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Worst MAE is material at -403.2, but broad structure or VWAP pruning risks removing too much profitable flow.

best_edges.edge: Fast charm-pin pullback edge with extended-VWAP winners
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1261.5 across 34 trades with median hold near 5 minutes and average MAE only -70.4, but owns the global -513.4 tail. Extended-VWAP winners are explicitly protected: 19 matches, $715.7 PnL, median MAE -15.3, median hold 2.27 minutes.

charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but $985.6 PnL, $197.1 average PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, and no slow >=12h trades. Prior admission-geometry follow-through improved objective and worst MAE from a weaker base.

breakout_long_1dte adds $1305 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with no slow >=12h trades. Prior direct quality tightening repeatedly hurt score and net profit, so the edge should be protected rather than tightened bluntly.

squeeze_long has 10 trades, $721 PnL, worst MAE -208.25, no slow trades, and strong option-map/order-book context. Prior convexity expansion was accepted, while later target compression hurt objective and worsened MAE.

broken_edges.evidence: Worst entry was charm_pin_pullback_long on 2026-06-26T12:36:24Z: MAE -513.4, hold 80m, ES-VWAP 26.69, VWAP acceleration -0.0179, pullback-from-high 9.25, rolling-high distance 14.25, RSI 42.42, ADX 17.95. Existing extended-VWAP rejection veto missed by only the max-vwap-distance boundary.
broken_edges.problem: Charm-pin high-extension tail is path-dependent and not cleanly separable by broad static thresholds.

broken_edges.action: repair with profile-local guard or split
broken_edges.evidence: pullback_long drives the book but owns -403.2 worst MAE and avg MAE -110.16. Bad-MAE rows include weak structure, low/negative higher-low or higher-high distance, slow first progress, and low setup quality, yet protected winners overlap these same dimensions.
broken_edges.problem: Pullback edge includes both recoverable weak-structure pullbacks and slow heat traps.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte is profitable, but direct tightening of structure, VWAP slope, forward DEX, and TP constraints hurt score by -355 to -594 and reduced net profit materially.
broken_edges.problem: 1DTE breakout quality gates are overfitting or cannibalizing profitable fallback trades.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE fallback contributes only $162.8 across 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale subset has 2 trades, $71 PnL, 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, 52.6 minute median hold, and -250.25 worst MAE.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback is low-efficiency in stale data states, but sample is too small and includes one strong EXPIRED_0DTE winner.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long are configured but produced zero trades in the current run.
broken_edges.problem: Inactive active-router profiles have no evidence in this sample and should not receive optimization budget unless used as explicit dormant/discovery controls.

market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over about 12.3 calendar days. Net profit is strong at $7149.6 with 107 completed trades, 100% win rate, 0 realized losses, drawdown around 0.4%, and 0 slow >=12h trades. The binding risk is hidden MAE and capital tie-up, not realized stop loss. The current leader plateau is stable across run_029/run_037/run_038, so next work should be surgical tail reduction with strict PnL retention. Broad filters are dangerous because protected fast winners and extended-VWAP winners overlap many bad-MAE features.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate delayed charm-pin recoveries from fast high-extension winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Overfits a small set of delayed recoveries or blocks profitable extended-VWAP fast winners.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: first +1.5/+2.0 profit time, MFE before MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, ES-VWAP extension, VWAP acceleration, rolling-high distance, profile id.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entry that fails to make early positive progress after entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -513.4 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total PnL >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Create a separate 1DTE pullback/reclaim route instead of tightening breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap cannibalizes profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion profile is unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY state, ES reclaim/pullback structure, option map availability, 1DTE profile attribution and cannibalization report.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.86, total net profit >=98% of leader, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use an existing dormant pin-avoid concept to split charm-pin/charm-wall tail states.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile adds no standalone trades, cannibalizes charm_pin_pullback_long, or blocks protected fast winners.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Router Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution across charm_pin_pullback_long, charm_wall_breakaway_long, and the activated dormant pin-avoid profile; protected fast-winner UTC retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm/pin context where current profiles enter near pin/high-extension rejection risk.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner retention >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Classify pullback deep-heat traps without damaging the main pullback engine.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Static proxies remove the highest-value pullback recoveries because weak structure is also present in profitable winners.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: pullback_long intratrade path timing, ES structure distances, setup quality, early MFE, ADX slope, and profile-level PnL attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entry with weak structure distances, low early progress, and delayed recovery behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and keeping total PnL/exposure hour flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Build evidence for the high-efficiency call-wall acceptance micro-edge.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Sample remains too small or local expansion admits low-quality wall-chase trades.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Isolated Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: call_wall_acceptance_long trade attribution, wall acceptance seconds/distance, call-volume increase, DEX/charm context, and per-profile MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with strong setup quality and bounded wall distance.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 total call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=250, worst MAE <= -150, and total net profit >=98% of leader.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge only the existing extended-VWAP rejection family around near-miss thresholds. Prioritize the smallest boundary study that catches the -513.4 charm-pin tail without broadly capping ES-VWAP extension. Validate explicitly against the 19 extended-VWAP protected winners.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce global worst MAE while preserving the fast extended-VWAP edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The global worst tail is not a new regime; it is a near miss of an already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection veto, especially around max VWAP distance and high-extension pullback geometry.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Tail Repair
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE improvement with total net profit >=98% of leader and protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95%.
next_experiments.risk: Boundary expansion can block clean high-extension winners and reduce the charm-pin edge more than the MAE reduction is worth.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use profile-aware charm-pin tail-risk box refinement around high ES-VWAP extension, high pullback-from-high, rolling-high distance, weak/nonpositive VWAP acceleration, low ADX/RSI, and existing veto warning points. Avoid global setup-quality floors.
next_experiments.goal: Catch the 80-minute charm-pin delayed recovery tail without cutting the 5-minute median charm-pin winner flow.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Charm-pin tail risk is concentrated in high-extension, weak-momentum pullback states with delayed path recovery; a narrow profile-specific box can reduce tail MAE with limited PnL loss.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Tail Box Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE, retained charm-pin PnL >=90%, total PnL >=98%, and fast_under_15m retention.
next_experiments.risk: The worst tail still closed profitable, so overblocking this box may reduce PnL while leaving other tails intact.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not repeat broad pullback TP/VWAP expansion. Test only local weak-structure/deep-heat pruning or a split-style holdout that targets slow-to-profit entries while protecting high-PnL pullback recoveries.
next_experiments.goal: Lower pullback_long tail MAE while preserving the book's largest PnL engine.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The pullback_long edge is real, but a subset of weak-structure, low-progress entries is responsible for excess heat; the main engine should be repaired locally rather than globally tightened.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Local Guard Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long PnL retention >=90%, pullback worst MAE improvement, total score, and total PnL/exposure hour.
next_experiments.risk: Weak structure is common in winners, so a blunt structure-score or VWAP-distance gate will likely over-prune.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate STALE_0DTE behavior from EXPIRED_0DTE and VALID_0DTE. Favor efficiency/quality holdout on stale no-0DTE profiles only; do not change valid-0DTE routing and avoid removing the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner unless separable.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce low-efficiency stale fallback exposure without damaging the main option-data-state edges.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE fallback trades add small PnL with long holds and some tail risk, so a state-local trim may improve risk-adjusted score if it does not leak into VALID_0DTE flow.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total net profit >=98%, and slow_12h = 0.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 stale trades and 1 expired trade make this fragile; a state-blind change can remove useful fallback insurance.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte baseline thresholds stable. If legal, add/activate pullback_long_1dte through the one-dte router as a companion attribution test; otherwise freeze 1DTE direct tightening.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE MAE quality through routing split rather than damaging the proven 1DTE breakout fallback.
next_experiments.hypothesis: 1DTE has a real $1305 edge, but direct tightening hurts; a companion 1DTE pullback split can separate fallback regimes without starving breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Attribution Test
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, total score, and breakout_long_1dte cannibalization.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte entries or the companion may produce too few trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze exits and require-breakout. Only test small admission-geometry follow-through around supported wall conditions; do not compress the 12-point charm-wall target.
next_experiments.goal: Preserve convex charm-wall contribution while preventing new tail from wall-chase entries.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Charm-wall breakaway remains a small but strong convex edge; supported admission tweaks may add or protect trades without worsening the global MAE.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Supported Admission Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/exposure >=225, total net profit >=98%, total worst MAE no worse than -513.4, and no slow trades.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample risk is high; admission expansion may dilute the edge or cannibalize existing charm-pin/pullback trades.

research_notes: The optimizer should not emit broad pruning experiments from single hidden signatures such as es_structure_rising_vwap or profile != breakout_long_1dte because those retain only ~79-82% PnL and do not remove the global worst MAE. The best risk-reducer path is to operate on near-miss guards and profile-local tail boxes. The current run is a leader plateau; acceptance should require both MAE improvement and very high PnL retention. Direct 1DTE tightening and squeeze target compression have already failed, while charm-pin tail-box repair, charm-wall admission geometry, and careful runtime-risk boundary work have been the accepted families.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment that reduces total net profit below 98% of the leader unless worst MAE improves dramatically below -403 and profile attribution supports promotion.
Protect fast_winners, low_mae_winners, high_pnl_winners, and extended_vwap_winners; require protected fast/extended winner retention reporting for any entry filter.
Do not use global setup-quality floors, broad ES-VWAP caps, or broad structure-score increases; protected winners overlap those features.
Keep slow_12h_trades at 0 and monitor exposure hours because this no-stop-loss strategy hides risk through hold time and MAE.
Treat STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE separately; stale fallback sample is too small for state-blind promotion.
Freeze inactive active-router profiles unless they are used as explicit dormant/discovery controls with router attribution.
